raw_consumer_statement:
What happened : I am disputing a fraudulent balance transfer that was moved from my former XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX to my current Citi credit card. The underlying debtoriginally {$12000.00} and now $ XXXXwas created through forged documents submitted by a third party named XXXX XXXX XXXX 

In XXXX I authorized a purchase for a property service that was never completed. When the merchant failed to deliver, I disputed the charge with XXXX as goods and services not provided. 
After that dispute, the merchant submitted a forged DocuSign lease agreement and completion report. These documents contain a copy-pasted image of my DocuSign signature and were never executed in my DocuSign account ; they have no valid audit trail or Certificate of Completion. 

Despite my police report ( XXXX XXXX Sheriff # XXXX ) and my FTC Identity-Theft Report, XXXX reinstated the charge using the forged paperwork. I later transferred the balance to Citi to secure a 0 % APR promotion while continuing to contest the fraud. The balance therefore originated from identity theft, forgery, and an unresolved billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ). 

XXXX has now closed its CFPB case ( # XXXX ) without producing verifiable authorization records such as the original DocuSign envelope metadata or audit trail. Citi now holds and reports this same fraudulent balance. 


Supporting documents provided : Police Report # XXXX FTC Identity-Theft Report ( XXXX ) XXXX XXXX XXXX DocuSign comparison XXXX correspondence ( XX/XX/XXXX ) Certified-mail receipts and timeline summary This complaint is not about payment delinquency but about a fraudulent transferred debt that originated from forged merchant documents. I am asking Citi to conduct a reasonable, verifiable investigation and remove the invalid balance if authenticity can not be proven.
